subject: Maths
source: Tamil Nadu Class 10 Mathematics 2025 textbook - textbook-style practice set

questions.q: If A = {1, 2} and B = {3, 4}, how many elements are in A x B?
questions.a: A x B has 2 x 2 = 4 ordered pairs. Correct answer: A. 4

questions.q: If f(x) = 2x + 3, find f(4).
Let f(x) = 3x - 1. Find f(5).
If f = {(1,2), (2,4), (3,6)}, write the domain and range.
If f(x) = x + 2 and g(x) = 2x, find (fog)(3).
Check whether {(1,3),(2,3),(3,4)} is a function.
questions.a: Each first element 1,2,3 has only one image. Therefore it is a function. It is many-one because 1 and 2 have the same image 3.

questions.q: If f(x)=2x+1 and g(x)=x-3, find fog and gof.
Let A={1,2,3}. If f:A→A is given by f={(1,2),(2,3),(3,1)}, check whether f is one-one and onto.
questions.a: Images are {2,3,1}. No two elements have the same image, so f is one-one. Range={1,2,3}=A, so f is onto. Hence f is bijective.

questions.q: Explain domain, codomain, range and function using f={(1,2),(2,4),(3,6)} from A={1,2,3} to B={2,4,6,8}.
questions.a: Given A={1,2,3}, B={2,4,6,8}, f={(1,2),(2,4),(3,6)}. Domain is the set of first elements: {1,2,3}. Codomain is the target set B={2,4,6,8}. Range is the set of actual images: {2,4,6}. Since every element of A has exactly one image in B, f is a function. It is not onto because 8 in B has no preimage.

questions.q: Find the 10th term of the AP 3, 7, 11, ...
questions.a: a=3, d=4. a10 = a+9d = 3+36=39. Correct answer: A. 39

questions.q: HCF of 24 and 36 is
questions.a: Factors common highest = 12. Correct answer: A. 12

questions.q: Find the 15th term of the AP 5, 8, 11, ...
Find the sum of first 20 natural numbers.
Find the sum of first 12 terms of AP 2, 5, 8, ...
Using Euclid division algorithm, find HCF of 65 and 117.
A milkman has 175 litres cow milk and 105 litres buffalo milk. Find the largest capacity container to measure both exactly.
questions.a: Largest container capacity = HCF(175,105). 175=105x1+70, 105=70x1+35, 70=35x2+0. HCF=35 litres.

questions.q: Find the 12th term from the end of AP -2, -4, -6, ..., -100.
questions.a: AP is -2,-4,...,-100. From the end, reverse AP is -100,-98,-96,... d=2. 12th term from end = -100 + 11(2) = -78.

questions.q: Find the sum of the first 25 odd natural numbers and prove the general result used.
questions.a: Sum of first n odd natural numbers = n². For n=25, sum = 25² = 625. General proof: 1=1², 1+3=4=2², 1+3+5=9=3². In general, nth odd number is 2n-1. Sum = n/2[2(1)+(n-1)2] = n/2[2+2n-2] = n².

questions.q: The roots of x² - 5x + 6 = 0 are
Degree of polynomial 3x² + 2x - 1 is
questions.a: Highest power is 2. Correct answer: A. 2

questions.q: Solve x² - 9 = 0.
Factorise x² + 7x + 12.
Solve x² - 7x + 10 = 0.
Find the GCD of x²-1 and x²-3x+2.
questions.a: x²-1=(x-1)(x+1). x²-3x+2=(x-1)(x-2). Common factor is x-1. GCD=x-1.

questions.q: Solve 2x² - 5x + 3 = 0 by factorisation.
Form a quadratic equation whose roots are 4 and -3.
questions.a: Sum of roots = 1. Product = -12. Equation: x² - (sum)x + product = 0. x² - x - 12 = 0.

questions.q: A rectangular park has perimeter 320 m and area 4800 m². Find its dimensions.
questions.a: Let length=l and breadth=b. 2(l+b)=320 so l+b=160. Area lb=4800. Let l=x, b=160-x. x(160-x)=4800. x²-160x+4800=0. Divide by 1: x²-160x+4800=0. Factor: discriminant = 160²-4(4800)=25600-19200=6400, √6400=80. x=(160±80)/2 gives 120 or 40. Dimensions are 120 m and 40 m.

questions.q: A tangent to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.
questions.a: True. This is the tangent-radius theorem.

questions.q: From an external point, how many tangents can be drawn to a circle?
questions.a: Two tangents can be drawn from an external point.

questions.q: If tangents PA and PB are drawn from point P to a circle, what is the relation between PA and PB?
questions.a: Tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal. Therefore PA = PB.

questions.q: State the tangent-radius theorem.
questions.a: The tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius drawn to the point of contact.

questions.q: A point P is 13 cm from centre O of a circle of radius 5 cm. Find tangent length PA.
questions.a: OA=5, OP=13, OA ⟂ PA. PA²=OP²-OA²=13²-5²=169-25=144. PA=12 cm.

questions.q: In similar triangles, if scale factor is 2:3 and smaller side is 8 cm, find corresponding larger side.
questions.a: Ratio smaller:larger = 2:3. Larger side = 8 x 3/2 = 12 cm.

questions.q: In triangle ABC, DE is parallel to BC. If AD=6 cm, DB=4 cm and AE=9 cm, find EC.
questions.a: By Basic Proportionality Theorem, AD/DB = AE/EC. 6/4 = 9/EC. 6EC = 36. EC = 6 cm.

questions.q: From point P, two tangents PA and PB are drawn to a circle. If PA=7 cm, find PB and justify.
questions.a: Tangents from an external point are equal. Therefore PA=PB. Given PA=7 cm, PB=7 cm.

questions.q: Prove that tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal.
questions.a: Let PA and PB be tangents from external point P to a circle with centre O, touching at A and B. Join OA, OB and OP. Radius is perpendicular to tangent, so OA ⟂ PA and OB ⟂ PB. In right triangles OAP and OBP, OA=OB (radii), OP is common, and both are right triangles. By RHS congruence, ΔOAP ≅ ΔOBP. Hence PA=PB.

questions.q: Distance between (0,0) and (3,4) is
Slope of line y = 2x + 5 is
Find the midpoint of A(2,4) and B(6,8).
Find slope of line joining (1,2) and (3,6).
Find distance between A(-1,2) and B(5,10).
Find equation of line with slope 3 and y-intercept 2.
questions.a: Equation y=mx+c. m=3, c=2. Therefore y=3x+2.

questions.q: Find equation of line passing through (2,3) and slope 4.
Find area of triangle with vertices (0,0), (4,0), (0,3).
questions.a: Area = 1/2 x base x height = 1/2 x 4 x 3 = 6 sq.units.

questions.q: Show that points (0,0), (4,0), (4,3), (0,3) form a rectangle and find its area.
questions.a: Let A(0,0), B(4,0), C(4,3), D(0,3). AB=4, BC=3, CD=4, DA=3 using distance formula. Slopes: AB=0, BC undefined, so adjacent sides are perpendicular. Opposite sides are equal and parallel. Hence ABCD is a rectangle. Area = length x breadth = 4 x 3 = 12 sq.units.

questions.q: sin 30° =
tan 45° =
Evaluate cos 60° + sin 30°.
If tan θ = 1 and θ is acute, find θ.
Evaluate sin²30° + cos²30°.
A pole casts shadow 10 m when angle of elevation of sun is 45°. Find height.
From a point 20 m away from a tower, angle of elevation is 30°. Find height of tower.
Prove that sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 for θ=60°.
A ladder 10 m long leans against a wall and makes an angle 60° with the ground. Find height reached on wall and distance from wall.
questions.a: Ladder is hypotenuse=10 m. Height = 10 sin60° = 10(√3/2)=5√3 m. Distance from wall = 10 cos60° = 10(1/2)=5 m. Therefore height reached is 5√3 m and foot is 5 m from wall.

questions.q: Volume of cylinder formula is
Curved surface area of cone is
Find volume of cylinder r=7 cm, h=10 cm. Use π=22/7.
Find CSA of cylinder r=3 cm, h=5 cm. Use π=22/7.
Find TSA of cylinder r=7 cm, h=10 cm.
Find volume of cone r=7 cm, h=12 cm.
A cylindrical tank has radius 3.5 m and height 4 m. Find its capacity.
Find surface area of sphere radius 7 cm.
questions.a: Surface area = 4πr² = 4 x 22/7 x 7 x 7 = 616 cm².

questions.q: A solid cylinder of radius 7 cm and height 10 cm is melted and recast into cones of radius 7 cm and height 5 cm. Find number of cones.
questions.a: Volume of cylinder = πr²h = 22/7 x 7 x 7 x 10 = 1540 cm³. Volume of one cone = 1/3πr²h = 1/3 x 22/7 x 7 x 7 x 5 = 770/3 cm³. Number of cones = 1540 ÷ (770/3) = 1540 x 3 / 770 = 6.

questions.q: Mean of 2, 4, 6 is
Probability of getting head in one coin toss is
questions.a: There are two outcomes H,T. Probability of head=1/2.

questions.q: Find range of 12, 18, 9, 25, 16.
A die is rolled once. Find probability of getting an even number.
Find mean of 10, 12, 14, 16, 18.
Find probability of drawing a red ball from a bag with 3 red and 5 blue balls.
questions.a: Total balls=8. Favourable red balls=3. Probability=3/8.

questions.q: Find variance of 2, 4, 6.
Two coins are tossed. Find probability of getting exactly one head.
questions.a: Sample space={HH,HT,TH,TT}. Exactly one head={HT,TH}. Probability=2/4=1/2.

questions.q: Find mean, variance and standard deviation of 2, 3, 5, 7, 8.
questions.a: Mean=(2+3+5+7+8)/5=25/5=5. Deviations: -3,-2,0,2,3. Squares:9,4,0,4,9. Variance=(26)/5=5.2. Standard deviation=√5.2≈2.28.
